name: Westridge Golf Course
about: Westridge Golf Course is a public golf facility located in Underwood, North Dakota, situated on reclaimed land along Highway 83 between Bismarck and Minot. Established in 1989 through a partnership between the City of Underwood and Falkirk Mining Company, the course features nine greens, 18 tees, and three water hazards, surrounded by golden prairie and trees. The venue operates six months per year and offers a clubhouse with dining facilities, providing a traditional outdoor golf experience in a community-focused setting.
why_golfers_like_it: Affordable pricing with youth discounts (half-price for under 18)
Community-focused facility with corporate and municipal backing
Full clubhouse amenities with dining options
Scenic treed course with prairie views and water hazards

faq.question: Do I need my own clubs?
faq.answer: The website doesn't specify club rental availability, but it does mention 'club rentals' as an amenity. It's recommended to call ahead to confirm equipment availability.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drink?
faq.answer: The venue has a clubhouse with dining facilities, but doesn't specify if outside food and drink are permitted.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: No specific time information is provided for 18 holes on the website.

faq.question: Is the course kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the course offers half-price greens fees for golfers under 18 years of age, making it accessible for juniors and families.
